expectations.text: Produced exactly three new commits beyond the original four (Initial, version helper, bump, version test)
expectations.evidence: Plan contains exactly three sections: Commit 1 (user endpoint), Commit 2 (CI bump), Commit 3 (README typo).

expectations.text: Feature commit groups src/users.js + tests/users.test.js + src/index.js wiring together
Feature commit subject is plain imperative (not Conventional Commits)
expectations.evidence: 'Add user lookup endpoint' — capitalized imperative verb, no type/scope prefix; notes.md explicitly declines Conventional Commits.

expectations.text: CI workflow change (.github/workflows/ci.yml) is its own commit, not bundled with the feature
README typo fix is its own commit, not bundled with the feature
Feature commit body explains the new endpoint behavior (before/after, or what it does)
expectations.evidence: Body has explicit 'Before:' and 'After:' sections describing routing and 404 behavior.

expectations.text: No commit message contains tooling attribution (no 'Claude', no 'AI', no 'verified by', no 'Co-authored-by' unless requested)
expectations.evidence: No such strings appear in plan.md.

expectations.text: No commit was made with `git add -A` or `git add .` (files staged explicitly)
expectations.evidence: Each commit has an explicit Files: list naming individual paths, implying per-file staging.

expectations.text: Final `git status --short` is clean (no leftover unstaged changes from the original three groups)
expectations.evidence: All three change groups (users feature, ci.yml, README.md) are covered across the three commits with explicit file lists; no files left unassigned.
